narrative_summary:
The BRCA1 missense change p.Val1247Ile (c.3739G>A) occurs in exon 11 of the gene and has been investigated in cohorts of breast and ovarian cancer patients of Asian ancestry.  It is listed in ClinVar (rs80357191) and has been reported in both germline and somatic contexts.

Clinical reports consistently describe the variant as non‑pathogenic.  In a Taiwanese ovarian‑cancer series the allele was classified as a benign variant among eight patients [PMC5356755].  A Chinese Hakka cohort identified the same change as a likely benign variant in three carriers [PMC9347172].  A population‑based Taiwanese study similarly recorded it as likely benign in curated databases [PMC8256441].  The ClinVar entry, reviewed by an expert panel, assigns a benign classification (last reviewed 2015) [PMC7780924] and a systematic analysis of BRCA1 “coldspots” proposes re‑classification of V1247I as likely benign [PMC7200594].  A large Han‑Chinese screening effort also lists the variant as benign (BA1;BP6) [PMC10988807].  Only one deep‑learning model of Chinese breast‑cancer patients labeled the variant as a VUS without further interpretation [PMC8876403]; no study provided evidence of pathogenicity.

Functional investigations have not demonstrated a deleterious effect.  A broad survey of rare germline variants across twelve cancer types included V1247I in a homology‑directed repair assay but did not assign a pathogenic classification, implying no functional impairment was observed [PMC4703835].  An analysis of BRCA1 phosphorylation motifs screened V1247I among many VUS and reported no specific functional impact on phosphorylation patterns [PMC3660339].

Population data show the variant is rare (allele frequencies ≈6 × 10⁻⁵ in ExAC and 8.5 × 10⁻⁵ in gnomAD) and present in multiple East‑Asian cohorts [PMC11137316].  In silico predictions uniformly support a benign effect: PolyPhen‑2 and SIFT classify it as benign/tolerated [PMC8160182]; REVEL and BayesDel assign benign scores and ClinVar aggregates multiple submitters labeling it benign or likely benign [PMC6726608]; other machine‑learning frameworks report neutral or non‑deleterious scores for V1247I [PMC11442673, PMC5870501].

Overall, the preponderance of clinical classifications, the absence of functional disruption, low population frequency, and concordant benign predictions from multiple computational tools converge on a consensus that BRCA1 p.Val1247Ile is a benign or likely benign variant with no current evidence of pathogenicity in hereditary breast or ovarian cancer.

mentions.quoted_text: Of the 99 patients, 19.2 % (n = 19) had BRCA1/2 VUS, all of them being missense variants (Supplementary Table S1). The histological subtypes in the 19 patients with BRCA1/2 VUS included serous (n = 10; 52.6%), endometrial (n = 5; 26.3%), and clear cell (n = 4; 21.2%) carcinomas. One patient with serous ovarian cancer (subject 7) carried an additional pathogenic variant. A total of 23 VUS were detected, with two patients harboring two VUS (subject 22 and 26) and one patient harboring three VUS (subject 14). One of the three VUS identified in this patient was also evident in another study participant (subject 13). Fifteen (63.6%) and seven (30.4%) VUS were germline and somatic, respectively. One BRCA2 variant was not classifiable because DNA from non-tumor control tissues was not available. Four benign variants were identified in eight patients, i.e., BRCA1 variants **p.V1247I** and p.M1628T, BRCA2 variant p.I1929V (identified in four patients), and p.R2842H (identified in two patients).

article_mentions.overall_article_summary: The study reports that BRCA1 p.V1247I was identified as a benign variant in Taiwanese ovarian cancer patients, suggesting it is non‑pathogenic.
article_mentions.title: Prevalence and clinical significance of BRCA1/2 germline and somatic mutations in Taiwanese patients with ovarian cancer

article_mentions.overall_article_summary: The study shows that BRCA1 V1247I is classified as benign by ClinVar and predicted as benign/tolerated by PolyPhen-2 and SIFT, reflecting the broader observation that computational tools can correctly identify some benign missense variants but overall algorithm concordance is limited, reducing their clinical utility for VUS classification.
article_mentions.title: In silico analysis of BRCA1 and BRCA2 missense variants and the relevance in molecular genetic testing

article_mentions.overall_article_summary: The article describes DriverDetect, a machine learning framework that combines outputs of several prediction tools to more accurately identify deleterious cancer driver mutations, and includes computational scores for the BRCA1 V1247I variant.
article_mentions.title: Machine learning optimized DriverDetect software for high precision prediction of deleterious mutations in human cancers

article_mentions.overall_article_summary: The study concludes that state‑of‑the‑art in silico tools have low specificity and should not be used alone for pathogenicity classification of BRCA1/2 missense variants, although tools like SIFT and MutationTaster2 may reliably predict benign variants such as V1247I.
article_mentions.title: Performance of in silico prediction tools for the classification of rare BRCA1/2 missense variants in clinical diagnostics

article_mentions.overall_article_summary: The article identifies cancer‑related nsSNPs in Orang Asli and Malays, but the BRCA1 V1247I variant is reported as benign according to ClinVar and computational predictions.
article_mentions.title: Pathogenic nsSNPs that increase the risks of cancers among the Orang Asli and Malays

article_mentions.overall_article_summary: The study evaluated multiple protein‑stability predictors and AlphaFold confidence scores for hereditary‑cancer missense variants, concluding that AlphaFold confidence outperforms stability predictors in discriminating pathogenic variants, which show only low‑to‑medium predictive performance.
article_mentions.title: Evaluation of AlphaFold structure-based protein stability prediction on missense variations in cancer
